workflowEn.label: Choose repeatable work
workflowEn.detail: Pick one recurring workflow with a strong example of the desired output.

workflowEn.label: Gather the pattern
workflowEn.detail: Attach examples, rules, documents, and commands that make the workflow successful.

workflowEn.label: Create the Skill
workflowEn.detail: Use $skill-creator to build and validate the Skill, including when it should trigger.

workflowEn.label: Test and refine
workflowEn.detail: Run the Skill on real work and add corrections wherever its output misses the standard.

checkEn: Test the Skill in one real chat and remove secrets or unsafe commands before sharing it.

workflowEn.label: Define success
workflowEn.detail: Set the target score, evaluation method, and stopping conditions.

workflowEn.label: Measure the baseline
workflowEn.detail: Run an eval on the current output and record scores and major weaknesses.

workflowEn.label: Improve one issue
workflowEn.detail: Fix the most important problem, then evaluate again to isolate its effect.

workflowEn.label: Repeat to the target
workflowEn.detail: Keep each iteration's history and continue until every criterion meets its threshold.

checkEn: Review both the scores and the real output; an eval may not cover every aspect of quality.

workflowEn.label: Set the reporting period
workflowEn.detail: Define the date range, audience, and what counts as a material change.

workflowEn.label: Gather core inputs
workflowEn.detail: Attach dashboards, KPI definitions, workbooks, forecasts, prior reports, and owner notes.

workflowEn.label: Validate and draft
workflowEn.detail: Check data quality, then summarize changes and evidence-backed drivers.

workflowEn.label: Confirm follow-ups
workflowEn.detail: Review numbers, sources, open questions, and owners before the meeting.

checkEn: Do not invent missing numbers; data owners must confirm material figures before presentation.

workflowEn.label: Set the scope
workflowEn.detail: Define the reporting period, audience, workstreams, and slides to update.

workflowEn.label: Attach the evidence
workflowEn.detail: Provide the prior pack, trackers, KPIs, forecast, cash flow, and owner commentary.

workflowEn.label: Build the narrative
workflowEn.detail: Connect progress, evidence, risks, and next targets inside the existing deck.

workflowEn.label: Inspect the pack
workflowEn.detail: Trace every number to its source and render slides to catch overflow or formatting issues.

checkEn: Data owners must confirm every key figure, assumption, and risk before the reporting pack is published.

workflowEn.label: Choose one finding
workflowEn.detail: Use an authorized report or ticket with clear scope and impact.

workflowEn.label: Confirm the issue
workflowEn.detail: Verify the vulnerable path, or prove it is already fixed, before changing code.

workflowEn.label: Make the smallest fix
workflowEn.detail: Use $codex-security:fix-finding for a focused patch and add a regression test.

workflowEn.label: Prove the result
workflowEn.detail: Test the original attack path and normal behavior, and document remaining uncertainty.

checkEn: Work only on authorized systems, and have a security expert review the patch and evidence before release.

workflowEn.label: Define the change set
workflowEn.detail: Choose the authorized PR, commit, branch diff, or working tree to review.

workflowEn.label: Name risk areas
workflowEn.detail: Highlight surfaces such as auth, user input, file paths, networking, or secrets.

workflowEn.label: Scan and validate
workflowEn.detail: Use $codex-security:security-diff-scan to separate evidenced flaws from suspicions.

workflowEn.label: Review before fixing
workflowEn.detail: Inspect affected lines, evidence, and proof gaps before opening separate remediation work.

checkEn: A person must confirm real reachability and impact before prioritizing or fixing each finding.

workflowEn.label: Verify the advisory
workflowEn.detail: Separate official-source facts from unconfirmed reports or commentary.

workflowEn.label: Set evidence criteria
workflowEn.detail: Define what would prove or disprove use of an affected package and version.

workflowEn.label: Audit read-only
workflowEn.detail: Inspect manifests, lockfiles, CI workflows, and scripts without installing or running lifecycle code.

workflowEn.label: Classify the evidence
workflowEn.detail: Mark results confirmed, needs review, or not affected, with severity and next steps.

checkEn: Do not run a possibly compromised package or script; seek approval before updates or credential cleanup.

workflowEn.label: Identify the meeting
workflowEn.detail: Provide the meeting name, date, attendees, and desired outcome.

workflowEn.label: Name allowed sources
workflowEn.detail: Point to the calendar event, documents, Slack, or email threads that may be used.

workflowEn.label: Build the prep brief
workflowEn.detail: Separate facts, gaps, agenda items, questions, and commitments you still owe.

workflowEn.label: Check suitability
workflowEn.detail: Remove unnecessary sensitive detail and fit the notes template to the audience.

checkEn: Check permissions and sensitive information before sharing; do not send messages or edit documents without approval.

workflowEn.label: Point to the change
workflowEn.detail: Provide the branch, PR, commit, or files that introduced the new behavior.

workflowEn.label: Find affected docs
workflowEn.detail: Search feature names, config, commands, and examples to identify impacted pages.

workflowEn.label: Edit only what changed
workflowEn.detail: Preserve structure, terminology, and links while keeping internal details out of public docs.

workflowEn.label: Verify every claim
workflowEn.detail: Run documentation checks and list the evidence for each user-facing behavior.

checkEn: Ensure unreleased roadmap details and internal customer information do not enter public documentation.

workflowEn.label: Define the starting point
workflowEn.detail: State whether the project is new or existing, plus devices and deployment target.

workflowEn.label: Create the build loop
workflowEn.detail: Scaffold SwiftUI and add a repeatable xcodebuild or Tuist script.

workflowEn.label: Build in slices
workflowEn.detail: Implement one feature at a time, reusing existing models, navigation, and utilities.

workflowEn.label: Test in Simulator
workflowEn.detail: Build, launch, and capture screenshots with an explicit scheme and Simulator.

checkEn: Verify the build and core flows in Simulator, including accessibility and target-device behavior.

workflowEn.label: Lock the KPI definition
workflowEn.detail: Set the formula, comparison period, expected direction, and decision this analysis supports.

workflowEn.label: Gather context
workflowEn.detail: Attach dashboards, exports, segment cuts, and relevant launch or campaign data.

workflowEn.label: Separate the drivers
workflowEn.detail: Analyze segments, cohorts, channels, or products and look for disconfirming evidence.

workflowEn.label: Conclude carefully
workflowEn.detail: Report confirmed drivers, hypotheses, caveats, open questions, and next actions.

checkEn: Do not treat correlation as causation; the metric owner must validate definitions and data before decisions.

workflowEn.label: Start with the decision
workflowEn.detail: Define the user, question, decision, and review cadence before choosing charts.

workflowEn.label: Gather definitions
workflowEn.detail: Attach the metric glossary, exports, example dashboards, and stakeholder feedback.

workflowEn.label: Draft the specification
workflowEn.detail: Define KPI hierarchy, charts, filters, owners, and when the data is trustworthy.

workflowEn.label: Plan monitoring
workflowEn.detail: Create a QA checklist, alert thresholds, and escalation boundaries before release.

checkEn: Test metric definitions and numbers against source data before calling the dashboard production-ready.

workflowEn.label: Set the reporting cadence
workflowEn.detail: Define the period, audience, and standard status language used each time.

workflowEn.label: Gather project records
workflowEn.detail: Attach trackers, initiative docs, KPIs, prior briefs, the decision log, and owner notes.

workflowEn.label: Find the delta
workflowEn.detail: Compare with the prior period and separate confirmed status from inference.

workflowEn.label: List required action
workflowEn.detail: Capture risks, decisions, next actions, owners, and stale items needing follow-up.

checkEn: The initiative lead should confirm statuses, owners, and open items before sharing with leadership.

workflowEn.label: Set the criteria
workflowEn.detail: Define the segment, time horizon, and weights for urgency, risk, or growth potential.

workflowEn.label: Gather customer signals
workflowEn.detail: Attach CRM exports, calls, emails, usage data, account plans, and review rules.

workflowEn.label: Rank with reasons
workflowEn.detail: Show evidence, risk, upside, missing context, and the next action for each account.

workflowEn.label: Approve follow-up
workflowEn.detail: Have account owners review rankings and draft messages before CRM updates or outreach.

checkEn: Account owners must confirm context and approve every message before contacting customers.

workflowEn.label: Set the forecast period
workflowEn.detail: Define the time window, deals in scope, and meanings of Commit and Upside.

workflowEn.label: Gather deal records
workflowEn.detail: Attach CRM, forecast snapshots, calls, emails, legal, support, usage, and owner notes.

workflowEn.label: Assess each deal
workflowEn.detail: Separate facts from inferred risks, with blockers and missing information.

workflowEn.label: Confirm before changing
workflowEn.detail: Review recommendations with the sales owner before updating the system of record.

checkEn: Do not judge confidence from stage alone; the sales owner must approve every forecast change.

workflowEn.label: Gather project inputs
workflowEn.detail: Bring together the brief, shared folder, notes, budget, member availability, and university rules.

workflowEn.label: Confirm current state
workflowEn.detail: Find the source of truth for dates, approvals, and budget before organizing the work.

workflowEn.label: Build the project hub
workflowEn.detail: Create milestones, an owner tracker, budget sheet, agenda, message drafts, and risk log.

workflowEn.label: Confirm with the team
workflowEn.detail: Agree on owners, dates, and expenses before editing files or sending messages.

checkEn: The team must confirm owners, dates, and budget before assigning work, messaging, or spending.

workflowEn.label: Extract the business question
workflowEn.detail: Separate the decision the requester faces from their suggested chart or answer.

workflowEn.label: Confirm data definitions
workflowEn.detail: Verify metrics, join logic, date ranges, sources, and missing values before analysis.

workflowEn.label: Run a first pass
workflowEn.detail: Analyze available data and build charts with caveats, sources, and open questions.

workflowEn.label: Have an analyst review
workflowEn.detail: Check calculations, charts, and conclusions before answering the requester.

checkEn: Do not assume metric definitions or join logic; an analyst must review the final result.

workflowEn.label: Define the decision
workflowEn.detail: Name the options, decision maker, deadline, criteria, and approval boundary.

workflowEn.label: Layer the evidence
workflowEn.detail: Keep internal evidence, external research, and interpretation separate.

workflowEn.label: Draft the decision memo
workflowEn.detail: Summarize the recommendation, alternatives, trade-offs, costs, risks, log, and open questions.

workflowEn.label: Challenge the recommendation
workflowEn.detail: Test assumptions and identify what would change the recommendation before the meeting.

checkEn: Verify every material fact and leave the final approval to the authorized decision maker, not the AI.

workflowEn.label: Compare plan and actuals
workflowEn.detail: Combine the plan, tracker, KPIs, model, notes, and owner updates to find the gap.

workflowEn.label: Separate causes and guesses
workflowEn.detail: Mark what is evidenced, what is likely, and what still needs confirmation.

workflowEn.label: Frame the options
workflowEn.detail: Summarize options, trade-offs, risks, owners, and decisions needed now.

workflowEn.label: Review with owners
workflowEn.detail: Have the project team confirm figures, causes, and recommendations before leadership review.

checkEn: Project owners must confirm facts and impact before changing the plan or system status.

workflowEn.label: Map the problem areas
workflowEn.detail: Find dead code, duplication, large modules, and legacy patterns, plus contracts to preserve.

workflowEn.label: Choose one theme
workflowEn.detail: Handle one concern at a time, such as dead code, control flow, or helper extraction.

workflowEn.label: Refactor in small rounds
workflowEn.detail: State current behavior, the structural change, and checks before each edit.

workflowEn.label: Prove behavior is stable
workflowEn.detail: Run the smallest relevant test and inspect the diff before the next round.

checkEn: Keep framework migrations, dependency upgrades, and public API changes as separate risk-controlled work.

workflowEn.label: Set the platform baseline
workflowEn.detail: Define iOS 26, Xcode 26, and fallback behavior for older devices.

workflowEn.label: Review one flow
workflowEn.detail: Find custom blurs, cards, buttons, sheets, and toolbars that should use system materials.

workflowEn.label: Move to native APIs
workflowEn.detail: Use glassEffect, GlassEffectContainer, and Glass styles only on suitable control surfaces.

workflowEn.label: Inspect real renders
workflowEn.detail: Build and capture key iOS 26 Simulator states, then test the fallback.

checkEn: Check readability, consistency, and older-device fallbacks; do not turn every surface into glass.

workflowEn.label: Choose the scene model
workflowEn.detail: Select a main window, Settings, MenuBarExtra, or utility window to fit the product.

workflowEn.label: Create a build loop
workflowEn.detail: Use xcodebuild or swift build in a repeatable build-and-launch script.

workflowEn.label: Build Mac-native UI
workflowEn.detail: Use sidebars, toolbars, menus, shortcuts, and an AppKit bridge only where needed.

workflowEn.label: Verify runtime behavior
workflowEn.detail: Build, launch, test the flow, and inspect logs before signing or notarization work.

checkEn: Verify windows, menus, keyboard use, accessibility, and real launch behavior before calling the feature ready.

workflowEn.label: Fix the environment
workflowEn.detail: Select the correct project, scheme, and Simulator and keep them consistent throughout.

workflowEn.label: Reproduce the issue
workflowEn.detail: Build, launch, and follow the real steps, reading the accessibility tree before tapping.

workflowEn.label: Collect evidence and fix
workflowEn.detail: Use screenshots, logs, or LLDB to find the root cause, then make a focused change.

workflowEn.label: Prove the fix
workflowEn.detail: Run the same path again and record the result on the same Simulator and scheme.

checkEn: Do not fix by guesswork; capture before-and-after evidence and test related behavior for regressions.

workflowEn.label: Choose a valuable action
workflowEn.detail: Pick a task users should complete without opening and tapping through the whole app.

workflowEn.label: Limit the entity surface
workflowEn.detail: Expose only the data the system needs to understand and pass the action.

workflowEn.label: Build intents and shortcuts
workflowEn.detail: Define App Intents, AppEntity, and AppShortcutsProvider with user-friendly names.

workflowEn.label: Test the handoff
workflowEn.detail: Build and verify the intent completes or opens the app on the correct screen.

checkEn: Review permissions and exposed data, then test every intent before publishing.

workflowEn.label: Choose one flow
workflowEn.detail: Select a window, sidebar, command, or sync path that code inspection cannot fully explain.

workflowEn.label: Add a Logger
workflowEn.detail: Use OSLog Logger with clear subsystem and category names at action boundaries.

workflowEn.label: Run and capture a trace
workflowEn.detail: Exercise the flow and filter events with Console or a log stream predicate.

workflowEn.label: Refine from evidence
workflowEn.detail: Move logs or fix code, then rerun until the timeline explains what happened.

checkEn: Never log secrets, tokens, personal data, or raw document contents.

workflowEn.label: Map the current system
workflowEn.detail: Inventory routing, data, auth, config, builds, tests, deployment, and external contracts.

workflowEn.label: Map the target stack
workflowEn.detail: Match old concepts to the new system and flag anything without a direct replacement.

workflowEn.label: Migrate by milestone
workflowEn.detail: Use a compatibility layer or move one module at a time with a rollback path.

workflowEn.label: Verify parity
workflowEn.detail: Run lint, type checks, tests, and side-by-side checks before the next phase.

checkEn: Avoid a big-bang rewrite and resolve failed validation before moving to the next milestone.

workflowEn.label: Set shared standards
workflowEn.detail: Gather learning outcomes, department requirements, and policies that apply to all sections.

workflowEn.label: Gather each section
workflowEn.detail: Attach syllabi, schedules, assignments, grading, LMS content, and instructor notes.

workflowEn.label: Find conflicts
workflowEn.detail: Separate required alignment from instructor discretion, citing sources and student impact.

workflowEn.label: Review with faculty
workflowEn.detail: Prioritize fixes and create a baseline checklist after instructors confirm each issue.

checkEn: Instructors must decide whether each difference is an error or an approved choice before source files change.

workflowEn.label: Select approved sources
workflowEn.detail: Gather current standards, pacing guides, calendars, prior lessons, and assessment guidance.

workflowEn.label: Link choices to sources
workflowEn.detail: Cite the document supporting each goal, lesson sequence, and assessment decision.

workflowEn.label: Draft the unit
workflowEn.detail: Create learning goals, lesson sequence, materials, formative checks, and misconceptions.

workflowEn.label: Have the teacher review
workflowEn.detail: Check standards, timing, assessment load, accessibility, and age fit before approval.

checkEn: Do not invent school requirements or student facts; the teacher must approve the plan before use.

workflowEn.label: Define the comparison
workflowEn.detail: Set versions, periods, materiality, sign convention, and financial lines in scope.

workflowEn.label: Attach finance inputs
workflowEn.detail: Combine actuals, budget, forecast, KPIs, expense tracking, cash view, and owner notes.

workflowEn.label: Build the driver bridge
workflowEn.detail: Rank drivers by impact, reconcile sources, and separate facts from hypotheses.

workflowEn.label: Close questions with owners
workflowEn.detail: Review source breaks, signs, and explanations with finance owners before reporting.

checkEn: Do not present a hypothesis as a conclusion; finance owners must confirm explanations before publication.

workflowEn.label: Confirm source content
workflowEn.detail: Use an approved lesson package, practice questions, feedback rules, and accessibility requirements.

workflowEn.label: Design a small first version
workflowEn.detail: Start with an introduction, guided example, practice, feedback, and final self-check.

workflowEn.label: Build and preview
workflowEn.detail: Use Sites for a simple experience and test both correct and incorrect answer paths.

workflowEn.label: Check accessibility
workflowEn.detail: Review keyboard use, labels, contrast, responsiveness, logic, and content before approval.

checkEn: The teacher must review content and accessibility for correct and incorrect paths before publishing or sharing.

workflowEn.label: Remove identifying data
workflowEn.detail: Strip names and personal information from submissions and grading exports.

workflowEn.label: Set the review scope
workflowEn.detail: Attach the rubric, course outcomes, calibration rules, and example feedback.

workflowEn.label: Build a calibration workbook
workflowEn.detail: Show score patterns, inconsistencies, strengths, gaps, and feedback themes with evidence.

workflowEn.label: Let faculty decide
workflowEn.detail: Review context and propose rubric clarification only from confirmed patterns.

checkEn: AI must not assign or change grades; faculty must judge every observation.

workflowEn.label: Preserve the original
workflowEn.detail: Attach the model and sources while keeping the original file for comparison.

workflowEn.label: Audit the structure
workflowEn.detail: Find risky formulas, hardcodes, broken links, circular references, period issues, and tie-outs.

workflowEn.label: Fix only clear issues
workflowEn.detail: Clean and repair formulas only when intent is certain, without changing business assumptions.

workflowEn.label: Deliver a QA memo
workflowEn.detail: Prioritize issues, name cells or tabs, and list questions for the finance owner.

checkEn: Never change business assumptions without approval; finance owners must review formulas and key outputs.

workflowEn.label: Set shared source material
workflowEn.detail: Use a reviewed lesson deck and unit plan with school guidance and accessibility needs.

workflowEn.label: Create the full pack
workflowEn.detail: Draft a teacher guide, student handout, answer key, family overview, and materials checklist.

workflowEn.label: Check consistency
workflowEn.detail: Compare terminology, examples, timing, instructions, and answers across every file.

workflowEn.label: Have the teacher approve
workflowEn.detail: Review accuracy, usability, accessibility, language, and policy before use.

checkEn: Keep every file as a draft until a teacher has reviewed accuracy, accessibility, language, and policy.
